(dragging = null)} />

Every song here is two numbers: how loud, how fast. So is your taste. Turn the amber taste arrow and watch each song's match get built: the loud product plus the fast product, stacked into one bar. Then grab Night Drive's round handle and change its size.

{#each rows as r (r.id)} {/each} {#if tied}tied{/if} {#if cheatVictim} ⚑ {cheatVictim.name} matches your taste better, but Night Drive outscores it on size {:else if shortChanged} ⚑ Night Drive matches best, but {leaderName} wins on size {/if}
{#if challenge === null && cheatActive}

Why this matters: {WHY.cheat}

{:else if challenge === null && shortChanged}

Why this matters: {WHY.short}

{/if}

Click any bar or arrow to inspect it. The dashed lines are that arrow's two entries (its loudness and its speed), the numbers the recipe multiplies.

the whole recipe, live for {sel.name}: loud {f(probe.x)}·{f(sel.x)} + fast {f(probe.y)}·{f(sel.y)} = {f(sel.px)} + {f(sel.py)} = {f(sel.score)} multiply the matched entries, add the products. The lilac slice of each bar is its loud product, the mint slice its fast product; stacked, they are the score.

lines: -      'A song that is simply louder and faster everywhere piles up score without matching your mix. A raw meter overrates intense tracks, so a recommender built on it keeps pushing whatever is biggest.',

+      "A candidate that is simply bigger everywhere piles up score without matching the probe's direction. A raw dot product overrates large vectors, so a system built on it keeps preferring whatever is biggest.",
-      'A quiet song that matches your mix exactly gets buried under bigger ones. Size is drowning direction, and fixing that is exactly where this lesson is headed.'
+      "A small candidate that matches the probe exactly gets buried under bigger ones. Size is drowning direction, and fixing that is exactly where this lesson is headed."
// one click always re-arms: a solved card resets on first click,

content.text: Now the axis labels and legend removal:

input.old_string:
<text x={CX + R + 18} y={CY - 6} class="axisq q-loud mono">loud</text>
				<text x={CX - 10} y={CY - R - 8} text-anchor="end" class="axisq q-fast mono">fast</text>
				<g class="legend">
					<line x1="16" y1="20" x2="34" y2="20" class="lg probe-stroke" />
					<text x="40" y="24" class="lgt mono">your taste</text>
					<line x1="16" y1="38" x2="34" y2="38" class="lg cand-stroke" />
					<text x="40" y="42" class="lgt mono">songs</text>
				</g>
input.new_string:
<text x={CX + R + 18} y={CY - 6} class="axisq q-loud mono" aria-hidden="true">loud</text>
				<text x={CX - 10} y={CY - R - 8} text-anchor="end" class="axisq q-fast mono" aria-hidden="true">fast</text>

content.text: Now the always-on candidate tip labels:

input.old_string:
<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft cand-stroke" />
						<polygon points={a.head} class="cand-fill" />
						{#if selected === r.id}
							{@const lp = labelPos(r.angle, r.len, 26)}
							<text
								x={Math.min(Math.max(lp.px, 44), S - 48)}
								y={Math.min(Math.max(lp.py, 16), S - 10)}
								class="lab song-lab halo">{r.name}</text>
						{/if}
					</g>
input.new_string:
<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft cand-stroke" />
						<polygon points={a.head} class="cand-fill" />
						{@const lp = labelPos(r.angle, r.len, 26)}
						<text
							x={Math.min(Math.max(lp.px, 44), S - 48)}
							y={Math.min(Math.max(lp.py, 16), S - 10)}
							class="lab song-lab halo"
							class:sel-lab={selected === r.id}
							aria-hidden="true">{r.name}</text>
					</g>

input.old_string:
<!-- probe on top -->
				{#if true}
					{@const a = arrow(probe.x, probe.y)}
					<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft probe-stroke" />
					<polygon points={a.head} class="probe-fill" />
				{/if}
				<circle
					cx={probeTip.px}
					cy={probeTip.py}
					r="22"
					onpointermove={onArenaPointer}
					class="handle probe-handle"
					class:active={dragging === 'probe'}
					onpointerdown={(e) => startDrag('probe', e)}
					onkeydown={probeKeys}
					role="slider"
					tabindex="0"
					aria-label="probe angle in degrees"
					aria-valuemin="0"
					aria-valuemax="360"
					aria-valuenow={Math.round(probeAngle)}
				/>
				<text x="14" y={S - 14} class="angle mono">taste at {Math.round(probeAngle)}°</text>
			</svg>
input.new_string:
<!-- probe on top -->
				{#if true}
					{@const a = arrow(probe.x, probe.y)}
					<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft probe-stroke" />
					<polygon points={a.head} class="probe-fill" />
				{/if}
				{@const plp = labelPos(probeAngle, 1, 30, 16)}
				<text
					x={Math.min(Math.max(plp.px, 44), S - 48)}
					y={Math.min(Math.max(plp.py, 16), S - 10)}
					class="lab probe-lab halo"
					aria-hidden="true">probe</text>
				<circle
					cx={probeTip.px}
					cy={probeTip.py}
					r="32"
					onpointermove={onArenaPointer}
					class="handle probe-handle"
					class:active={dragging === 'probe'}
					onpointerdown={(e) => startDrag('probe', e)}
					onkeydown={probeKeys}
					role="slider"
					tabindex="0"
					aria-label="probe angle in degrees"
					aria-valuemin="0"
					aria-valuemax="360"
					aria-valuenow={Math.round(probeAngle)}
				/>
				<text x="14" y={S - 14} class="angle mono" aria-hidden="true">probe at {Math.round(probeAngle)}°</text>
			</svg>

					<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft probe-stroke" />
					<polygon points={a.head} class="probe-fill" />
				{/if}
				<circle
					cx={probeTip.px}
					cy={probeTip.py}
					r="22"
					onpointermove={onArenaPointer}
					class="handle probe-handle"
					class:active={dragging === 'probe'}
					onpointerdown={(e) => startDrag('probe', e)}
					onkeydown={probeKeys}
					role="slider"
					tabindex="0"
					aria-label="probe angle in degrees"
					aria-valuemin="0"
					aria-valuemax="360"
					aria-valuenow={Math.round(probeAngle)}
				/>
				<text x="14" y={S - 14} class="angle mono">taste at {Math.round(probeAngle)}°</text>
			</svg>
tool_use_result.newString:
<!-- probe on top -->

					{@const a = arrow(probe.x, probe.y)}
					<line {...{ x1: a.shaft.x1, y1: a.shaft.y1, x2: a.shaft.x2, y2: a.shaft.y2 }} class="shaft probe-stroke" />
					<polygon points={a.head} class="probe-fill" />
				{/if}
				{@const plp = labelPos(probeAngle, 1, 30, 16)}
				<text
					x={Math.min(Math.max(plp.px, 44), S - 48)}
					y={Math.min(Math.max(plp.py, 16), S - 10)}
					class="lab probe-lab halo"
					aria-hidden="true">probe</text>

content.text: Checking one Svelte syntax risk: `{@const}` must live inside a block context, not directly under a plain element like `<svg>`. Let me fix the probe label placement to sit inside the existing `{#if true}` block.

content.text:
This resolves the compile-safety concern. The revision is complete.

DONE Revised DotAlignment.svelte: renamed candidates c1..c4 (dropped song-name flavor text/story references to match the literal id-based visual spec), added an always-visible "c1".."c4" tip label per candidate plus a new "probe" label on the probe arrow (tangentially offset so it never collides with a coincident candidate label), removed the corner legend, marked all decorative SVG text `aria-hidden="true"`, switched score readouts (bar numbers, aria-labels, and the arithmetic strip's total) to one-decimal precision with id-based aria-labels ("c2 score 0.9" form), enlarged both drag-handle hit circles from r=22 to r=32 for reliable 44px touch targets at 375px width, and added an explicit `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` block to kill the pulse animation.

result:
This resolves the compile-safety concern. The revision is complete.

DONE Revised DotAlignment.svelte: renamed candidates c1..c4 (dropped song-name flavor text/story references to match the literal id-based visual spec), added an always-visible "c1".."c4" tip label per candidate plus a new "probe" label on the probe arrow (tangentially offset so it never collides with a coincident candidate label), removed the corner legend, marked all decorative SVG text `aria-hidden="true"`, switched score readouts (bar numbers, aria-labels, and the arithmetic strip's total) to one-decimal precision with id-based aria-labels ("c2 score 0.9" form), enlarged both drag-handle hit circles from r=22 to r=32 for reliable 44px touch targets at 375px width, and added an explicit `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` block to kill the pulse animation.
